name: Transmurciana 09 and Cuatro Calas Loop
name_original: Castillo de San Juan de Águilas y playas de Calarreona — vuelta desde Águilas
description: Cycle the moderate 16.1-mile Transmurciana 09 and Cuatro Calas Loop, featuring coastal views and inland paths near Águilas.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Águilas offer a diverse landscape, ranging from undulating coastal paths with Mediterranean views to trails through arid plains and the Sierra de Almenara. The region is characterized by its 28 kilometers of coastline, including protected areas like the Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre Regional Park. Cyclists can experience varied topography, from sea-level routes to ascents into the hills. Best touring cycling routes around Águilas The most popular touring cycling route is Cala Blanca and Playa de la Galera - circular from Calabardina through the Regional Park Cabo Cope and Puntas de Calnegre, a 14.1 miles (22.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This route explores the protected coastal landscapes of the Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre Regional Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Transmurciana 09 and Cuatro Calas Loop, a moderate 16.1 miles (25.9 km) path. This route combines a section of the Transmurciana trail with coastal views around the Cuatro Calas area. Local touring cyclists also love the Cala Cristal Views – Coastal Path With Sea Views loop from Águilas-El Labradorcico, a 48.7 miles (78.4 km) trail leading through coastal paths with expansive sea views, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Águilas is defined by its Mediterranean coastline, arid plains, and the hills of Sierra de Almenara. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely coastal rides to more demanding routes. The routes in Águilas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 2400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Águilas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Águilas?
A: Águilas offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 100 different options to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging ascents into the Sierra de Almenara.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Águilas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Águilas feature diverse terrain. You'll find undulating coastal paths with stunning Mediterranean views, trails through arid plains, and more demanding ascents into the hills of the Sierra de Almenara. The region's 28 kilometers of coastline also include protected areas like the Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre Regional Park, offering varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Águilas?
A: Águilas, part of Spain's "Costa Cálida," boasts an average of 320 sunny days per year and temperatures ranging from 17 to 32 degrees Celsius. This makes it an ideal destination for cycling throughout much of the year, particularly in spring, autumn, and even winter, when the climate is mild and pleasant.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Águilas?
A: Yes, Águilas offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a leisurely ride. These often include flatter coastal paths or routes with minimal elevation gain, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without significant physical challenge. Approximately 25 of the routes are classified as easy.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in Águilas?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists looking for a challenge, Águilas provides routes with more significant ascents and longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40376224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Cristal Views – Coastal Path With Sea Views loop from Águilas-El Labradorcico&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.7-mile (78.4 km) trail with over 550 meters of elevation gain. The region's varied topography, including the Sierra de Almenara, ensures plenty of demanding options.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Águilas?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Águilas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016753483/cala-blanca-and-playa-de-la-galera-circular-from-calabardina-through-the-regional-park-cabo-cope-and-puntas-de-calnegre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Blanca and Playa de la Galera - circular from Calabardina through the Regional Park Cabo Cope and Puntas de Calnegre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016764434/transmurciana-09-and-cuatro-calas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transmurciana 09 and Cuatro Calas Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes in Águilas?
A: Cycling routes in Águilas often pass through stunning natural areas. You can explore the protected landscapes of the Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre Regional Park, featuring hidden coves and pristine coastline. Notable landmarks include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/535410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower&lt;/a&gt;, and beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cocedores Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4858540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galera Beach&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer panoramic views, such as those from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6033937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Coast and Mountains near Cabo de Gata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by bike in Águilas?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Águilas offers cultural points of interest that can be integrated into your cycling tours. The San Juan Castle provides panoramic views over the town and coastline. The Hornillo Pier, an old iron structure used for mineral loading, is another photogenic historical landmark often found along coastal routes. The town itself features charming whitewashed buildings and the Plaza de España.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Águilas?
A: Yes, Águilas is a great destination for family cycling. Many coastal paths and sections within the town are relatively flat and safe, making them suitable for families with children. The town's "Cycling Friendly Águilas" initiative also promotes signposted itineraries designed for various skill levels, including easier options.

Q: What are the options for parking when cycling in Águilas?
A: While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, Águilas is noted for having low-traffic, well-maintained roads, and as a cycling-friendly town, you can generally find parking in and around the town center or near popular starting points for routes. Many routes begin from accessible locations like Águilas-El Labradorcico or Calabardina, where parking is typically available.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists in Águilas?
A: Águilas has local public transport options, but specific provisions for carrying bicycles on buses or trains may vary. It's advisable to check with local transport providers directly for their current policies regarding bikes, especially if you plan to use public transport to reach a starting point or return from a route.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the cycling routes in Águilas?
A: Águilas is a bustling Spanish fishing town with a beautiful promenade lined with bars and restaurants, perfect for a post-ride relaxation. While specific cafes directly on every trail are not guaranteed, many routes pass through or near the town and coastal villages where you can find refreshments. The town's commitment to bike tourism suggests a welcoming environment for cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Águilas?
A: The touring cycling experience in Águilas is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 260 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, ranging from stunning Mediterranean coastline to the challenging Sierra de Almenara, and the well-maintained, low-traffic roads. The variety of routes catering to all abilities is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.
tips: If we are in the Águilas area, it is worth going to the Castillo San Juan de Águilas, a beautiful and historic place, whose views of the town and the Mediterranean Sea are spectacular! I recommend buying the ticket (€2 and students and children free) to go up to the top or enjoy the guided tour.
It is a very quiet walk by the sea in the town of Águilas with beautiful and picturesque views throughout the route.

Beach located in natural environment. 5 km from what is known as Cuatro Calas, a magnificent beach that, together with its sisters, has an important extension of golden sands that gently submerge into a transparent sea.

faqs.question: What is the actual route covered by the "Transmurciana 09 and Cuatro Calas Loop" tour?
faqs.answer: Despite the name, this specific komoot tour primarily focuses on the scenic coastal Cuatro Calas Loop near Águilas. It's a 25.87 km touring bicycle route with about 199 meters of elevation gain, rated as moderate. The Transmurciana 09 is a much longer and more challenging inland mountain bike stage, which is not the primary focus of this particular tour.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows well-worn coastal paths with very few significant slopes, characteristic of the Cuatro Calas Loop. It's suitable for touring bicycles and offers a relaxed ride along the coastline.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy stunning coastal views and pass by four beautiful coves: Calarreona, La Carolina, Cala Cerrada, and La Higuerica Beach. The route also offers views of the volcanic outcrop of Punta Parda and the historic Castillo de San Juan de las Águilas, providing a magnificent panorama of Águilas.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cuatro Calas Loop section of this tour is generally considered easy and accessible, with very few slopes or elements of difficulty. It's suitable for all skill levels and families looking for a relaxed, scenic ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round. However, for the most comfortable experience, consider visiting during spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, so if cycling then, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trails within the Paisaje Protegido de Cuatro Calas. However, they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and ensure the safety of other users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access the Paisaje Protegido de Cuatro Calas?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits required or entrance fees to access the Paisaje Protegido de Cuatro Calas for recreational activities like cycling. It is a protected natural area, so please respect the environment by staying on marked paths and not disturbing flora or fauna.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: You can find parking available near the coastal areas of Águilas, particularly around the beaches and coves that form part of the Cuatro Calas Loop, such as near Calarreona or La Higuerica Beach. Specific parking areas may vary, but street parking or designated lots are usually available in these popular coastal spots.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. The most significant portions include the Transmurciana 09 (53% of the route), Sendero del Mediterráneo - Murcia (37%), and Camino del Alto Guadalentín (23%). It also briefly touches on Camino Las Palomas and Transmurciana 08.

faqs.question: What amenities are available near the route, especially in Águilas?
faqs.answer: Águilas is a well-equipped coastal town. You'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, shops, and accommodation options. There are also public facilities and services available, particularly along the Paseo de la Constitución and other central areas.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the coastal nature and potential for sun exposure, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and comfortable cycling attire. A basic bike repair kit and a charged phone are also recommended. While the terrain is generally easy, wide tires with good grip are beneficial for comfort on varied paths.
